Interface IBuiltInRuleApp

All Superinterfaces:
RuleApp
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractAuxiliaryContractBuiltInRuleApp, AbstractBlockContractBuiltInRuleApp, AbstractBuiltInRuleApp, AbstractContractRuleApp, AbstractExternalSolverRuleApp, AbstractLoopContractBuiltInRuleApp, BlockContractExternalBuiltInRuleApp, BlockContractInternalBuiltInRuleApp, CloseAfterMergeRuleBuiltInRuleApp, ContractRuleApp, DefaultBuiltInRuleApp, InfFlowBlockContractInternalRule.InfFlowBlockContractInternalBuiltInRuleApp, InfFlowLoopInvariantBuiltInRuleApp, IsabelleRuleApp, JmlAssertBuiltInRuleApp, LoopApplyHeadBuiltInRuleApp, LoopContractExternalBuiltInRuleApp, LoopContractInternalBuiltInRuleApp, LoopInvariantBuiltInRuleApp, MergeRuleBuiltInRuleApp, OneStepSimplifierRuleApp, SetStatementBuiltInRuleApp, SMTRuleApp, UseDependencyContractApp
@NullMarked public interface IBuiltInRuleApp extends RuleApp

  • Method Details

    • rule

      BuiltInRule rule()
      returns the built-in rule of this rule application
      Specified by:
      rule in interface RuleApp

    • tryToInstantiate

      IBuiltInRuleApp tryToInstantiate(Goal goal)
      Tries to complete the rule application from the available information. Attention: Do neither add GUI code to the rules nor use this method directly Instead ask the implementation of the UserInterfaceControl to complete a built-in rule. Returns a complete app only if there is exactly one contract. If you want a complete app for combined contracts, use forceInstantiate instead. For an example implementation see e.g. UseOperationContractRule or UseDependencyContractRule.

    • forceInstantiate

      IBuiltInRuleApp forceInstantiate(Goal goal)

    • getHeapContext

      List<LocationVariable> getHeapContext()

    • isSufficientlyComplete

      boolean isSufficientlyComplete()
      returns true if tryToInstantiate is able to complete the app
      Returns:
      true if tryToInstantiate is able to complete the app
